data.title: Petco Health and Wellness delivers on profitability goals, raises EBITDA target for the year
data.description:
Petco Health and Wellness Company (WOOF) is rallying in Wednesday’s premarket as the company reported a profit for the second quarter in a row and raised its profit outlook for the year by $6M at the midpoint.

Shares are up by double-digits and expected to open above their 100- and 200-day moving averages.

“Rebuilding the base of our economic model has been a priority in 2025. This strengthened base sets the foundation for a return to growth during fiscal 2026,” said Joel Anderson, Petco's Chief Executive Officer.

Although sales were down in the third quarter – in line with the company’s forecast – profitability continued to improve with adjusted earnings of $0.03 per share, up from a loss of $0.06 per share in the same quarter last year and 4 cents better than expected. Adjusted EBITDA increased by more than $17M to $98.6M while operating income was up $25M to $29.2M.

Despite a 3.3% drop in sales to a below-consensus $1.46B, the company trimmed its COGS by 4% contributing to a 75-basis point increase in its gross profit margin to 38.9%.

For the current quarter, net sales are expected to be down low single digits from the same quarter last year while adjusted EBITDA is seen within a range of $93M and $95M, down from $96.1M in Q4 of 2024.

For the full year, net sales are expected to be down 2.5% to 2.8%, translating into a range of $5.93B and $5.95B versus $5.97B estimates.

Adjusted EBITDA, however, is now expected to be within a range of $395M to $397M versus the earlier guidance of $385M to $395M.

data.title: Zscaler tumbles, even as Wedbush calls results, guidance 'robust'
data.description:
Zscaler (ZS) shares fell 6% in premarket trading on Wednesday, even as the cybersecurity company reported better-than-results and guidance, which Wedbush Securities referred to as “robust.”

“ZS delivered its FY1Q26 results featuring beats on the top and bottom lines while also raising its FY26 guidance across the board as demand for the company’s product portfolio remains strong driven by continued strength in its new Cloud and AI solutions,” analysts at the firm, led by Dan Ives, wrote in a note to clients.

They continued: “Overall, we continue to view ZS as a premier name to own in the cybersecurity industry, as it remains very well-positioned in the continued shift to Zero Trust/AI security for enterprises.” The analysts have an Outperform rating and $350 price target on Zscaler.

For the quarter ended October 31, Zscaler earned an adjusted $0.96 per share as revenue rose 26% year-over-year to come in at $788.1M. Analysts had expected adjusted earnings of $0.86 per share on $773.86M in sales.

Looking ahead, Zscaler expects second quarter revenue to range from $797M to $799M, above the $796.1M consensus. It projects second quarter adjusted earnings to range from $0.89 to $0.90, compared to the $0.89 per share estimate.

Full-year revenue is expected to range from $3.28B to $3.3B, above the $3.27B estimate. Adjusted earnings for the full-year are expected to be between $3.78 to $3.82 per share, above the $3.65 per share estimate.

data.title: JBS merges its leather business with Viva to create a new corporate entity
data.description:
JBS N.V. (JBS) agreed to merge its leather division with Brazilian leather group Viva by combining the two companies’ leather production and commercialization assets into a new jointly controlled entity called JBS Viva. 

Under the structure outlined in a securities filing, the new company will be owned 50% by JBS (JBS) and 50% by Viva's shareholders, Vanz Holding and Viposa, making it a true 50-50 venture.

JBS via is expected to be a large-scale leather platform, with the capacity to process more than 20 million hides per year, operating through about 31 factories. Notably, the merger positions the new entity among the largest players in the global leather industry. 

Looking ahead, JBS (JBS) will appoint the chairman of the board and the chief financial officer of the new entity, while Viva’s shareholders will select the chief executive officer and chief operating officer. Strategically, the merger is seen as allowing JBS to more fully monetize hides from its meatpacking operations through a specialized, scaled leather business, while Viva gains secure upstream raw material access and broader international reach.

The deal cannot close until closing conditions are met and regulators finish their review of the implications on competition.

Shares of JBS (JBS) were up 1.5% in premarket trading on Wednesday after gaining 1.6% on Tuesday.

data.title: Altus Group launches $350M substantial issuer bid
Fastly to transfer listing to Nasdaq
data.description:
• Fastly (FSLY) plans to voluntarily transfer its stock exchange listing from the New York Stock Exchange to the Nasdaq Global Select Market.
  • The company expects to begin trading as a Nasdaq-listed company on or about December 9, 2025, and will continue to trade under its current ticker symbol “FSLY”.
  • “We’re excited to join Nasdaq and be listed among the most innovative and leading technology companies in the world,” said Kip Compton, CEO at Fastly. “This is one of several steps in our current transformation as we scale Fastly while working to unlock long-term value for customers and shareholders.”
  • FSLY +1.2% premarket.

data.title: Elekta AB reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Elekta AB press release (EKTAF): Q2 GAAP EPS of SEK 0.60 (vs. 0.55 prior)
  • Revenue of SEK 4.07B (-6% Y/Y)
  • The book-to-bill ratio was 1.0 (0.99), rolling twelve months ended at 1.09 (1.09).
  • Higher adjusted gross margin of 37.9 percent (35.7).
  • Adjusted EBIT amounted to SEK 411 M (423), corresponding to a margin of 10.1 percent (9.8).

data.title: Workday falls as analysts mull investors' concerns over M&A driven growth
data.description:
Shares of Workday (WDAY) fell about 6% premarket on Wednesday after analysts cited investor concerns over current remaining performance obligations ((cRPO)) growth being driven by mergers and acquisitions, or M&A.

The stock was the biggest decliner among the S&P 500 premarket on Wednesday.

Morgan Stanley kept its Equal-weight but raised the price target on the stock to $280 from $255.

"An outsized beat in cRPO likely gets discounted by investors for being M&A driven. While an accelerated M&A strategy well leverages core assets – customer base, data, distribution – and adds to the AI toolkit, it does little to improve investor sentiment on the durability of growth," said analysts led by Keith Weiss.

The analysts noted that Workday printed a "solid" third quarter result, with 12-month subscription backlog (cRPO) coming in 180 basis points, or bps, ahead of consensus and growing 17.6% year-over-year (up from 16.3% growth in the second quarter).

"However, as the conference call proceeded, and analysts and investors began piecing together the inorganic contributions to the quarter and the guide, the perceived positive momentum of the print quickly faded. Within the quarter, the recently closed Paradox acquisition added over a point to the cRPO balance, leaving the organic growth just above the high-end guidance, and when we adjust for the short-term tenant impacts cRPO growth looks to have actually decelerated slightlyto 14.7% YoY, from 14.9% in Q2 and 16.5% in Q1," said Weiss and his team.

The analysts noted that looking forward into the fourth quarter, while the subscription guidance came up by about $4M, the move was entirely from the recently closed Sana acquisition (which also adds around $20M to cRPO in the fourth quarter), while the operating margin target of 28.5% fell slightly short of consensus.

In addition, the analysts said that, when talking about strength of AI solutions in the quarter, CEO Carl Eschenbach noted a 1.5% point positive impact from AI SKUs, but this is largely driven by acquired products like HiredScore and Evisort (both 2024 deals).

"Bottom-line, for bears, looking to discount the pick-up in cRPO growth seen in the quarter, M&A and the inclusion of the tenant revenues give plenty of fodder," said Weiss and his team. "However, investors should keep an eye on the potential for this portfolio of acquired AI-based modules to add momentum to the business over time," the analysts added.

Jefferies maintained its Buy rating on Workday's shares with a $325 price target. 

"F3Q broadly beat, with cRPO of 17.6% 2pts ahead of ests, though F4Q cRPO guide of 15-16% failed to impress investors when excluding impact from onetime items. Against the backdrop of ongoing AI displacement fears impacting the apps layer, we highlight the sticky nature of WDAY's business, margin upside potential (high 20s vs peers in low 40s), and solid mgmt. [management] team," said analysts led by Brent Thill.

The analysts said they liked —  the public sector and the state, local, and education, or SLED, momentum; AI momentum continues to grow; and big step up in stock buyback.

However, the analysts noted areas to watch for include — lighter than expected fourth quarter cRPO guidance, and continued headwinds for institutions reliant on federal funding.

Wells Fargo kept its Overweight rating on the shares but lowered the price target to $290 from $300.

"FQ3 results were mostly as-expected, though growth metrics remains a touch 'noisy' as M&Adrove (most of) cRPO/rev upside in still-tough backdrop," said analyst Michael Turrin and his team.

data.title: Calian Group Non-GAAP EPS of $1.00, revenue of $203M
data.description:
• Calian Group press release (CLNFF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.00.
  • Revenue of $203M (+12% Y/Y)
  • Gross margin at 34.0%, in line with last year
  • Adjusted EBITDA up 2% to $24 million (margin of 11.9%)
  • Operating free cash flow1 of $17 million, representing a conversion of 72%
  • New contract signings of $122 million, bringing FY25 signings to over $1.1 billion
  • Achieved 15% year-over-year revenue growth in defence end market solutions

data.title: HP is being negatively impacted by AI-induced 'memory supercycle,' analysts warn
data.description:
HP's (HPQ) weaker-than-expected guidance is due in part to the rising cost of memory from the artificial intelligence boom, something that is not being seen by its competitors, such as Dell (DELL), Wall Street analysts said.

Shares fell 3% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

“HPQ’s guide reflects more significant/material memory cost headwinds vs. peers (Lenovo, DELL) that recently reported, though we see potential for upside vs. their prudent outlook if impacts are less severe than anticipated,” Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani wrote in a note to clients. Daryanani kept his In-Line rating on HP but lowered his price target to $25 from $29 after the results.

Companies like Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), Sk hynix (HXSCF), Seagate (STX), Western Digital (WDC), Micron (MU), Sandisk (SNDK) and others are pushing through price increases for memory, in some cases, by as much as 30% to 50%, amid the demand for AI and high performance computing. While that has benefited these companies and is likely to do so for some time, it's clearly hurting HP, analysts pointed out.

“Memory price inflation starts to rear its ugly head, but we believe risks are still biased downward from here,” Morgan Stanley analyst Erik Woodring wrote in a note to investors.

“And we can empathize with how difficult guiding for the next 12 months must be at this point – memory contract prices are moving 50%+ higher this quarter alone, supply of both DRAM and NAND will be limited in CY26, suppliers are [reneging] on long-term contracts, and clean room capacity is nearly non-existent. Said differently, while there is a historical memory supercycle playbook to work off of, this is an unprecedented memory supercycle.” Woodring has an Underweight rating on HP and lowered his price target to $20 from $21.

Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers said the memory headwind could be worth as much as $0.30 per share in lost earnings for HP in fiscal 2026, given that memory and storage account for roughly 16% to 18% of total PC unit cost.

“We think HP could look to pass [through] price increases; however, demand inelasticity at 5%+ could provide challenges,” Rakers added. Rakers has an Underweight rating on HP and a $25 price target.

data.title: Blue Ant Media to acquire Thunderbird Entertainment for $89 million
data.description:
• Blue Ant Media (BAMI:CA) has entered into a definitive arrangement to acquire Thunderbird Entertainment Group (TBRD:CA) in a cash and stock deal for $89 million.
  • Under the terms of the arrangement agreement, each Thunderbird shareholder will have the option to elect to receive, for each Thunderbird share, (i) 0.2165 Blue Ant subordinate voting shares (ii) $1.77 in cash, or (iii) a combination thereof, subject to rounding and proration based on a maximum cash consideration of $40 million.
  • Upon completion of the transaction existing Blue Ant and Thunderbird Shareholders are expected to own ~79% and 21% of the pro forma company, respectively. If no cash elections are made, existing Blue Ant and Thunderbird shareholders would own approximately 67% and 33% of the pro forma company, respectively.
  • The acquisition is expected to be immediately accretive to earnings per Blue Ant SVS. Blue Ant expects to realize cost synergies of $7 million in the first 12 months.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of calendar 2026.
  • Source: Press Release
